description: Minimal risk. Most people can stay outside safely with little to no protection.
Minimal risk. Most people can stay outside safely with little to no protection.
Moderate risk of harm from unprotected sun exposure. Wearing sunglasses and sunscreen is recommended.
Moderate UV radiation. Skin may begin to burn after prolonged exposure without protection.
High risk of skin damage. Sunscreen, protective clothing, and shade are recommended.
Very strong UV radiation. Unprotected skin may burn quickly. Limit sun exposure.
Very high UV levels. Skin damage can occur rapidly without protection.
Very high risk of sunburn. Protective measures are strongly recommended.
Extremely strong UV radiation. Avoid direct sun exposure during midday hours.
Extreme UV radiation. Unprotected skin can burn within minutes. Maximum protection required.
Extreme danger. Avoid sun exposure between 10am¨C4pm whenever possible.
Extremely dangerous UV radiation. Skin damage can occur very quickly. Stay indoors or fully protected.
